name: Medulla Oblongata
location: Lowest segment of brainstem extending from pons down to the foramen magnum.
description: Contains vital autonomic centers controlling cardiac rate, blood pressure, and respiratory rhythm, along with reflex centers for swallowing, coughing, and vomiting.
functions: Cardiac center regulation & blood pressure control
clinicalImportance: Contains the Pyramidal Decussation where 90% of motor nerve fibers cross to the opposite side of the body.
researchNotes: Pre-Bötzinger complex in the medulla generates the rhythm of respiration.
interestingFacts: Explains why the left hemisphere of the brain controls motor movement on the right side of the body.
